# InfiniEdit

> Browser-based, client-side save editor for the game Infinite Craft. Open, edit, analyze, and export `.ic` save files â€” elements, recipes, dependency graph â€” entirely in the browser, with optional live recipe lookups. Unofficial fan project, not affiliated with Infinite Craft or its creators.

InfiniEdit is a single-file, no-build, no-framework HTML/CSS/JS app. There is no backend for core functionality: no accounts, no analytics, no server-side storage of save data. It also exposes its editing actions as WebMCP tools (`document.modelContext` / `navigator.modelContext`) so an in-browser AI agent can inspect and edit the loaded save directly â€” call `get_save_summary` first, then `search_elements` / `get_element` to look things up, and `add_element` / `add_recipe` / `delete_element` / `export_save` to make changes.

## Docs

- [Full AI-oriented reference](https://infiniedit.qzz.io/): request with `Accept: text/markdown` for a complete Markdown doc covering data model, localStorage keys, external API calls, feature list, keyboard shortcuts, and the full WebMCP tool schema (inputs/outputs) â€” same URL, content-negotiated, URL bar unchanged.
- [Source code](https://github.com/QikSeekDev/InfiniEdit): full client implementation, single `index.html`.
- [Issues / contribution](https://github.com/QikSeekDev/InfiniEdit/issues): bug reports and pull requests.

## Optional

- [Mirror](https://infiniedit.vercel.app): identical app, alternate deployment.
- [InfiniBrowser API](https://infinibrowser.wiki/api/Recipe?id=example): third-party recipe database InfiniEdit optionally queries for autocomplete and missing-recipe lookups (element names only, no save data sent).
- [WebMCP spec](https://webmachinelearning.github.io/webmcp/): the in-browser tool-calling standard InfiniEdit's AI tools are built on.
